The B100A sensor is not rejecting double or misaligned product. Maintenance is checking it

Maintenance has adjusted the tolerance of this sensor to detect more of the twisted pieces to prevent jams. So far, it has done well to kick these off. 


This sensor is not designed to detect or kick off doubles

We were down about 10-15 mins mostly figuring out what the issue was. We removed 10 molds from the twisting area then ran it towards that area to scrape it off
TG4 is getting linked pieces, maintenance is here assisting
After demolding area,had a lot of chocolate build up in vibration are causing molds to sound. Created a gap to clean area

Kimberly Morales Alan Greer

Was it causing jams or just making loud sounds?
Loud noises, at first we thought it could’ve been loose bolts as it’s happened before. It wasn’t until we created the gap we verified what it was

Thank you for the detail Kimberly Morales. 

Did you get any video you could post?

Once creating the gap what did the team verify the root cause was?
Just build up?

We didn’t record, maintenance was there assisting as well. 
Root cause was built up pushing molds up, we’ve seen that before during shift change. 

Suggestion could be to clean it once a day during shift change.

We could look to do it during the capture test once per day and run the gap intentionally ahead of time.

Clustering (doubles, triples, etc) is the biggest defect at the moment
Additionally, the biggest concern with doubles and triples is that once they go through bagging and shipping, they break apart, and expose the cookie and/or cream inside
That especially be the case on white chocolate, but it will also happen with Milk
